normalized_interpretation: The rich text editor is not a small note editor; it is the unified Handshake authoring surface that replaces the earlier separate Monaco editor plus plain text editor idea.
The editor must work side by side with the Handshake Obsidian clone / Loom wiki surface, not as a later unrelated add-on.

The project knowledge index must solve model context rot by making project code, specs, work packets, taskboard state, changelogs, workflow artifacts, and research retrievable through typed machine-readable entities and relationships.
The canonical storage target is PostgreSQL. Markdown, HTML, wiki pages, editor exports, generated context bundles, and debug evidence reports are projections, not the durable source of truth.

Legacy Project Brain, Semantic Catalog, RAG retrieval-mode, AI-ready evidence/export, relationship_id graph retrieval, and ContextPack recorder-visibility stubs are now folded into WP-KERNEL-009; do not activate those older stubs separately.

Where older folded stubs conflict with this WP, WP-KERNEL-009 wins, but deterministic retrieval, evidence export, relationship IDs, Project Brain runtime visibility, and ContextPack recorder visibility must not be lost.

All product-code legacy wording for ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual must migrate to the canonical stuck-together UserManual product term, with only explicit deprecated compatibility shims allowed where needed for safe migration.
Future implementation should care more about correct product behavior against this stub, the master spec, and product code reality than about repo-governance paperwork polish.

draft_scope.intent:
- What: Build Handshake Project Knowledge Index, native memory system, Loom/Obsidian replacement surface, UserManual-aware no-context operating layer, and HS_Rich_Text_Editor as one integrated product foundation.
- Why: Handshake must let local and cloud models recover project state without chat memory, navigate the codebase/spec/WPs through backend APIs, and preserve operator intent across session limits.
- Native dependency stance: Core behavior must not rely on outside apps, outside services, external daemons, Docker-only paths, SQLite fallback, or sidecar research papers. Allowed external runtime inputs are models/GGUF/tensors and explicitly gated CUI portable artifacts. Tiptap and Monaco are accepted as bundled Handshake product libraries, not external apps.
- Source-of-truth stance: Research papers and GitHub projects are context only. Accepted ideas must be translated into this WP, official MTs, and later Master Spec enrichment so future models can work from SPEC_CURRENT and project artifacts alone.
- Product goal: Every managed project can be indexed into PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed typed knowledge, rich documents, source spans, claims, memory layers, retrieval traces, and UserManual pages with visual-debug and backend navigation evidence.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE:
  - PostgreSQL/EventLedger-canonical ProjectKnowledgeIndex for sources, entities, edges, spans, claims, passages, wiki projections, context bundles, rich documents, UserManual pages, retrieval traces, and validation receipts.
  - Handshake-native memory system translated from research into MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictDetection, ConflictResolution, EvidenceSpan, BridgeEdge, and Ranking contracts.
  - Tiptap/ProseMirror as canonical rich document engine and Monaco as canonical embedded code-block/code editor, fully packaged and tested inside Handshake.
  - Loom/Obsidian replacement workflows: backlinks, local/global graph, tags, tag hubs, folders, folder color labels, pins, unlinked mentions, project links, generated wiki projections, source citations, and graph-bias visibility.
  - Code/source indexing with Handshake-managed Tree-sitter parsers first, plus optional SCIP/LSP-style import format as owned projection/import, not outside service dependency.
  - PDF/media/research ingestion with extraction receipts, transcript/OCR metadata, failure states, repair queues, and no silent partial indexing.
  - UserManual naming migration and build-time UserManual updates for every touched feature/tool/primitive/UI/API.
  - Local/cloud swarm backend navigation, worktree/workspace claims, leases, receipts, state recovery, visual-debug evidence, and validator-ready HBR matrix.
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - Making sidecar papers, markdown vaults, generated wiki pages, external apps, Docker containers, SQLite databases, Redis/Valkey caches, provider traces, or browser UI state authoritative.
  - Shipping realtime multi-user collaboration servers as a prerequisite. CRDT semantics for human/AI draft state and EventLedger promotion are in scope; external sync servers are not required for core proof.
  - Depending on CUI portable for core WP-009 behavior. It may remain an explicitly gated artifact/input class for other owning packets.
  - Whole-project governance refactor beyond what is required to index, cite, validate, and later enrich the Master Spec.
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria:
- AC-001: Stub-level only until activated; Coder and Validator may not start from this stub directly.
- ACTIVATION-AC-001: Refinement confirms SPEC_CURRENT v02.191 indexed bundle as current Master Spec authority and identifies stale spec text to patch.
- ACTIVATION-AC-002: Refinement translates all accepted external research into WP/spec-native technical clauses so no future MT depends on opening sidecar papers.
- ACTIVATION-AC-003: Refinement locks Tiptap/ProseMirror plus Monaco as bundled Handshake product libraries and rejects outside editor apps/services.
- ACTIVATION-AC-004: Refinement confirms PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, no SQLite-in-Handshake, no Docker-default proof, and no mock-only proof.
- ACTIVATION-AC-005: Refinement defines CRDT/EventLedger semantics for rich document drafts, AI edit proposals, graph mutation proposals, and conflict repair.
- ACTIVATION-AC-006: Refinement defines native PDF/media/research ingestion receipts, transcript/OCR metadata, partial-extraction handling, repair queues, and secret redaction.
- ACTIVATION-AC-007: Official MT contracts are promoted directly from microtask_plan_draft, with no separate MT execution surface or suite field.
- ACTIVATION-AC-008: Every official MT contains scope, files, dependencies, constraints, expected behavior, acceptance criteria, verification, HBR focus, and handoff requirements for a no-context local model.
- PRODUCT-AC-001: A managed project indexes configured roots into PostgreSQL/EventLedger with source hashes, typed entities, edges, spans, claims, passages, and receipts.
- PRODUCT-AC-002: Rich documents roundtrip through Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON, PostgreSQL/EventLedger, CRDT draft state, projections, and visual-debug selectors.
- PRODUCT-AC-003: Monaco code blocks are embedded in Handshake, persist through backend save/load, expose code navigation, and require no external editor/server.
- PRODUCT-AC-004: Memory claims use probationary/stable/rejected/superseded/conflicted lifecycle with evidence spans and conflict-resolution receipts.
- PRODUCT-AC-005: Retrieval chooses explicit modes and emits QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace evidence that can reconstruct context bundles.
- PRODUCT-AC-006: Loom/Obsidian navigation exposes backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, search, wiki projections, and repair states backed by canonical records.
- PRODUCT-AC-007: UserManual replaces legacy ModelManual naming in product code except for declared deprecated compatibility shims with tests.
- PRODUCT-AC-008: Local/cloud swarm lanes can navigate backend state, claim workspaces, coordinate leases, recover after compaction, and attribute actions.
- PRODUCT-AC-009: Visual-debug evidence proves editor, Loom, UserManual, retrieval-debug, graph, and error states are readable, navigable, and non-overlapping.
- PRODUCT-AC-010: Negative fixtures block false PASS for secrets, stale indexes, partial PDF extraction, bad citations, contradictory memory, parallel write conflicts, missing PostgreSQL, SQLite regressions, and external dependency regressions.
draft_scope.governance_artifact_stance:
- The operator has explicitly said future work packets will not rely on repo governance as the primary correctness target. Treat this stub as a launch record, not as product truth.
- Correct product behavior against the master spec, current product code, and this operator intent outranks paperwork polish.
- This stub still uses current governance conventions so it can be found by existing tools, but implementation must not optimize for generating more paperwork instead of building the product substrate.

patterns_found: Project memory should be layered: exact source spans, typed entities and edges, candidate claims, evidence links, conflict handling, generated wiki projections, retrieval traces, and context bundles.

MemGraphRAG adds construction-time memory governance: candidates stay probationary until schema, fact, passage evidence, conflict handling, and promotion rules make them safe for active graph retrieval.
Generated wiki pages help only as cited projections with freshness metadata; they must not become unchecked authority.
Tree-sitter is the practical first layer for code structure; SCIP/LSP can be optional precision import later.
Agent memory systems are useful references, but Handshake durable memory must live in its own PostgreSQL/EventLedger control plane.
Obsidian-like value comes from backlinks, links, local graph, folder labels, tags, fast navigation, and recoverable broken links.
Rich editor saved state should be structured document/block data; Markdown/HTML/wiki/context bundles remain projections.
Parallel model work requires backend navigation routes, typed attribution, leases, cancellation, process ownership, visual/debug evidence, and UserManual entries.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse memory retrieval_mode, progressive_retrieval, bitemporal, scoring, trace_export, and trace_export_postgres surfaces.
Reuse kernel/context_bundle.rs and workflows.rs for bundle and ContextPack lifecycle behavior.
Reuse Loom migrations/storage/API for block graph, backlinks, search, and local graph behavior.
Reuse TiptapEditor, DocumentView, and WorkspaceSidebar for the rich editor slice.
Reuse model_manual/UserManual no-context harness and projection infrastructure while migrating canonical naming to UserManual.
Reuse internal visual debugger, inspector_read, DebugPanel, and tests/visual harnesses.
Reuse managed_postgres, no-SQLite tripwire, CRDT modules, sandbox/workspace materializer, swarm orchestration, and role mailbox routes.
rejected_options: Directly copying MemGraphRAG runtime/storage: rejected; only its memory-governed graph-construction ideas are adapted.
Markdown-only vault as canonical authority: rejected because Handshake needs typed machine-readable recovery across code/spec/workflow/media/rich-doc/model routes.
Vector database only: rejected because vector search loses exact spans, typed relationships, freshness, conflicts, and deterministic explainability.
Graph-only without source spans: rejected because generated relationships need citations and repair paths.
Valkey/Redis as canonical memory: rejected for this WP; authority belongs in PostgreSQL/EventLedger.
SQLite anywhere in Handshake storage/tests/fixtures/caches/examples/harnesses/temporary adapters: rejected by current authority.
Docker Desktop or Docker Compose as default PostgreSQL proof: rejected; proof must use Handshake-managed or explicit real PostgreSQL.
Separate Monaco editor plus separate note editor: rejected in favor of HS_Rich_Text_Editor with embedded code editor nodes.
A separate MT execution surface or suite field: rejected; this edit splits the existing microtask_plan_draft entries directly.
Leaving MemGraphRAG or any paper as a sidecar dependency for future implementers.
Leaving editor framework selection open after WP-009 activation; Tiptap plus Monaco is the intended native stack.
Using an external editor app, external graph database, external RAG service, external sync server, Docker-only service, SQLite cache, markdown vault, or provider trace as WP-009 authority.
selected_approach: Use current Master Spec SPEC_CURRENT v02.191 as authority, then enrich it later with WP-009 native contracts.
Use Tiptap/ProseMirror as the canonical rich document engine and Monaco as the embedded code editor, both bundled into Handshake.
Use PostgreSQL/EventLedger as canonical state for knowledge, memory, rich documents, retrieval traces, UserManual, and validation receipts.

Translate accepted memory-system research into Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictDetection, ConflictResolution, BridgeEdge, and Ranking contracts without relying on the paper as future authority.
Use Tree-sitter as the first code parser family and keep SCIP/LSP-style precision as an owned import/projection path.
Use visual debugging and backend navigation as required validation surfaces for no-context models and parallel agents.
risks: Overbuilding graph infrastructure before a vertical slice proves value.
Generated wiki pages or inferred edges gaining authority without evidence.
Index staleness after file moves, worktree changes, branch changes, taskboard changes, or migrations.
Rich editor save-format churn causing data loss.
Legacy ModelManual naming staying mixed with UserManual.
Hybrid retrieval becoming the default and weakening authority boundaries.
Parallel agents corrupting shared editor/index state or losing attribution.
Visual/debug proof being skipped because backend tests pass.
Docker/SQLite/mock-only proof creeping in because it is convenient.
mitigations: Lock the first vertical slice and require end-to-end proof before broadening coverage.
Keep generated claims probationary until evidence, conflict state, and promotion rules allow active graph use.
Store source hashes, index runs, stale markers, evidence links, and trace exports in PostgreSQL.
Version rich document schemas and prove save/load/export round trips.
Make UserManual naming migration explicit with search/tests and declared compatibility shims.
Require retrieval mode, non_hybrid_reason, relationship_id, QueryPlan, and RetrievalTrace evidence.
Require HBR-SWARM, CRDT, and backend navigation proof for shared state and parallel lanes.
Require internal visual debugger or headless capture for editor/wiki/debug UI.
Require no-SQLite/no-Docker-default tripwires and real PostgreSQL/EventLedger proof.
validation_plan: Parse the WP contract and confirm microtask_plan_draft contains small-model-ready MT entries, not a child workflow.
Run governance projection/build-order/taskboard checks after contract edits.

Later implementation must run real PostgreSQL migration/storage tests, no-SQLite tripwire, retrieval-mode tests, graph ID stability tests, ContextBundle/ContextPack trace tests, UserManual no-context tests, CRDT replay tests, and visual/debug GUI tests.
Validators must reject isolated mocks, Docker/SQLite default proof, missing visual evidence, missing UserManual updates, and unresolved HBR rows.
research_basis.translation_rule: External papers, GitHub repositories, docs, and discussions may explain why WP-009 exists, but they are not source of truth. Every accepted idea must be restated in WP-009 fields, official MT contracts, and later Master Spec enrichment before implementation may rely on it.

expected_reuse: PostgreSQL primary control plane and no-SQLite tripwire stay authoritative.
AI-ready data indexing/retrieval provides existing ingestion and hybrid retrieval concepts.
Loom MVP provides existing block/graph/search semantics to extend into Obsidian-like navigation.
Artifact System provides durable media and evidence materialization for embeds.
FEMS and ContextPacks provide model-facing memory/bundle patterns.
Flight Recorder provides traceability for indexing runs, bundle compilation, and editor save failures.

Existing Tiptap/Yjs frontend dependencies are product-code anchors to internalize as built-in Handshake editor/CRDT subsystems; canonical document truth remains PostgreSQL/EventLedger and browser/editor state is never authority.
Existing model_manual/manual product code is legacy naming and must migrate to UserManual during this work packet.
Monaco is not yet present in the WP-005 app dependency manifest and must be added as a bundled Handshake code-node subsystem, with worker bundling and offline proof.
UserManual coverage must include dependency boundary, built-in editor stack, PDF/media ingestion, secrets/permissions, CRDT recovery, backend navigation, and session-limit state restoration.
WP-005 visual debugger surfaces must be reused to inspect editor, Loom, retrieval trace, graph, UserManual, and error states introduced by WP-009.

architecture_seed.system_name: Handshake Project Knowledge Index + Loom Rich Editor

hard_constraints: No markdown vault as canonical authority.
No Valkey/Redis canonical memory for this work packet.
Every generated summary/claim must be traceable to source entities/spans or explicitly marked unsupported.

No outside runtime dependencies for core WP-009 behavior except operator-provided model files, GGUF/model artifacts, tensors/safetensors, and explicitly gated CUI portable artifacts owned by their own WP.
Tiptap/ProseMirror and Monaco must be bundled, lockfile-governed, offline-capable Handshake product libraries/assets, not external editor apps, CDNs, or manually launched services.

Research papers, GitHub repositories, docs, and discussions are non-normative inputs only; WP-009, official MTs, and later Master Spec enrichment must contain the complete Handshake-native requirements.
CRDT draft state must reconcile through PostgreSQL/EventLedger promotion and cannot become a separate browser-local or external-sync authority.
PDF/media/research ingestion must record extraction receipts, partial-failure state, transcript/OCR metadata, and repair queues before content can support stable memory claims.

core_components.name: Project Source Registry
core_components.purpose: Register managed project roots, source files, source kinds, content hashes, modified times, and index eligibility.

core_components.name: Knowledge Entity/Edge/Span Store
core_components.purpose: Persist typed entities and relationships with source spans and evidence.

core_components.name: Code Indexer
core_components.purpose: Use Tree-sitter first to extract symbols, imports, definitions, comments, tests, and structural spans; leave room for SCIP/LSP precision later.

core_components.name: Spec/Governance/Workflow Artifact Indexer
core_components.purpose: Index specs, work packets, microtasks, taskboard rows, changelogs, workflow JSON, receipts, and research notes as project knowledge inputs.

core_components.name: LLM Wiki Compiler
core_components.purpose: Generate cited project wiki pages/topic summaries from indexed sources and mark them stale when sources change.

core_components.name: Loom/Obsidian Projection Surface
core_components.purpose: Expose notes, wiki pages, backlinks, local graph, folder/project navigation, tags, color labels, and search.

core_components.name: HS_Rich_Text_Editor
core_components.purpose: Unified rich text, code, media, link, and project authoring surface.

core_components.name: Context Bundle Compiler
core_components.purpose: Compile bounded, cited, task-specific context bundles for models with no prior conversation context.

core_components.name: Retrieval Debug / Evidence View
core_components.purpose: Show why a bundle or wiki page contains each source, span, edge, and summary.

core_components.name: Project Brain Runtime Bridge
core_components.purpose: Expose Project Brain query/citation/context-compaction flows as deterministic Project Knowledge Index runtime paths with QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace evidence.

core_components.name: Semantic Catalog Backend Index
core_components.purpose: Persist and query catalog entries as backend routing contracts for tools, retrieval, and Spec Router planning.

core_components.name: Retrieval Mode Policy Engine
core_components.purpose: Select none/direct_load/exact_lookup/graph_traversal/hybrid_rag and record non_hybrid_reason for cheapest authoritative retrieval.

core_components.name: AIReady Evidence Export Bridge
core_components.purpose: Bound export evidence for index artifacts, rebuild/update lifecycle events, provenance, retention, and portability.

core_components.name: ContextPack Recorder Bridge
core_components.purpose: Emit bounded recorder events for pack build/reuse/refresh/freshness decisions with pack/source hashes.

core_components.name: UserManual Migration Surface
core_components.purpose: Rename legacy ModelManual product surfaces to UserManual while preserving declared compatibility shims.

core_components.name: Handshake Dependency Boundary / Built-In Runtime Surface
core_components.purpose: Enforce native-by-default runtime behavior, bundled-library ownership, allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable exceptions, and validator tripwires against outside apps/services.

core_components.name: Built-In Tiptap/ProseMirror Editor Core
core_components.purpose: Own rich document schema, custom nodes, CRDT draft bridge, save/load, projection, visual-debug selectors, and UserManual coverage inside Handshake.

core_components.name: Built-In Monaco Code Node Core
core_components.purpose: Own embedded code block editing, worker bundling, language registration, diagnostics, code navigation, and persistence without external editor services.

core_components.name: PDF/Media Ingestion and Evidence Adapter
core_components.purpose: Detect text layers, transcript/OCR needs, partial extraction, source hashes, media timing anchors, repair queues, and evidence eligibility.

core_components.name: Secrets and Permissions Gate
core_components.purpose: Prevent unauthorized roots, secrets, credentials, machine-local paths, and denied files from entering indexes, context bundles, wiki projections, logs, or UserManual.

core_components.name: CRDT State Recovery and Conflict Ledger
core_components.purpose: Record actor/site IDs, draft updates, conflict state, promotion decisions, replay plans, and crash/session recovery receipts.

core_components.name: Native Research Translation Registry
core_components.purpose: Track which external research ideas were accepted, rejected, translated into WP/MT/spec language, and later patched into SPEC_CURRENT.

hs_rich_text_editor.product_role: The single Handshake authoring surface for rich text, code, notes, specs, research, workflow artifacts, and media-linked project knowledge.
hs_rich_text_editor.relationship_to_monaco: Tiptap/ProseMirror owns the unified rich document experience. Monaco is embedded inside Handshake as the canonical language-aware code-block/code editing surface. Neither may be treated as an external editor app or outside service.

canonical_save_format.selected: PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed structured Tiptap/ProseMirror document JSON plus stable Handshake block/entity IDs, CRDT draft-state references, schema versions, and projection metadata.
locked_decisions: Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document editor framework for WP-009.
Monaco is the canonical embedded code-block/code editor for WP-009.
Rich document authority is not a Markdown vault, browser-local state, or generated wiki page.
remaining_activation_checks: Confirm exact package versions and worker bundling in the WP-005 product codebase.
Confirm document tables reuse Loom concepts or introduce sibling tables without duplicating authority.
Confirm visual-debug selectors and backend APIs before Coder starts.

required_features: Supports many coding formats and language-aware code blocks.
Can create code blocks automatically from fenced code, pasted code, language hints, indentation, or slash-command selection.
Can embed videos by stored asset, URL, or artifact reference.
Can embed albums as ordered media asset groups.
Can embed slideshow references using syntax like [[HS_slideshow]].
Can insert pictures using syntax like [[HS_images]] and drag/drop or paste flows.
Can link files like Obsidian-style wikilinks.
Can link folders and apply folder color labels.
Can link projects and project entities.
Can show backlinks and local graph relationships for current document/entity.
Can expose stable IDs/selectors for frontend model-driven testing.
Can surface retrieval-mode and source-evidence state for linked project entities when a model or operator inspects the document.
Can open or link UserManual pages without using legacy ModelManual wording.
auto_codeblock_rules_seed: Triple-backtick paste creates a code-block node with language if specified.
Large pasted text with high code-likeness can prompt or automatically wrap as code depending on operator preference.
Indented multi-line text can suggest a code block but must not silently rewrite prose.
Language detection confidence must be visible or reversible.
The user can convert code block back to plain text without data loss.
Code block node stores language, raw text, display settings, lint markers if present, and source link if pasted from an indexed file.
embed_rules_seed: Embeds store references to artifact/media/source IDs, not random absolute filesystem paths.
External URLs may be stored but should be explicitly typed and previewed safely.
Missing media/embed targets render as repairable broken-link nodes, not blank UI.
Albums and slideshows are ordered collections with stable item IDs and optional captions.
Large image/video collections must reuse artifact storage and lazy loading.

delivery_guidance: Do not split WP-009 into sidecar workflows during activation; use MT naming groups inside the existing microtask_plan_draft and official MT contracts.
Do not split the rich editor away and forget it; it is the operator intended Monaco/text-editor replacement integrated with Tiptap rich documents.
Do not build the Obsidian replacement as a standalone Markdown vault; it must sit on the same PostgreSQL/EventLedger knowledge substrate as project indexing.
Do not reactivate folded older stubs separately; preserve their detail inside WP-009 MTs and deprecated links.
Do not let retrieval-mode policy collapse into generic hybrid RAG; exact/direct/graph/passage-fallback modes are first-class outcomes.
Do not let external papers remain required reading; translate their value into Master Spec enrichment seeds and MT contracts.
Do not ship product code using ModelManual, Model Manual, or model_manual as canonical wording; the canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual.

microtask_plan_draft.title: ResearchSpecTranslation-001-CurrentSpecEntrypointAudit
microtask_plan_draft.scope: Verify SPEC_CURRENT, v02.191 manifest, module resolver, and changelog are the current Master Spec authority and record exact anchors for WP-009 enrichment. This MT belongs to ResearchSpecTranslation. Convert research, operator intent, and current authority into self-contained WP/spec-ready requirements.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Research papers and GitHub/codebase findings are context inputs only; translate every accepted idea into Handshake-native requirements before activation.
Activation must cite SPEC_CURRENT and the v02.191 indexed bundle as current authority, not the v02.182 monolith alone.
microtask_plan_draft.expected_behavior: CurrentSpecEntrypointAudit leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CurrentSpecEntrypointAudit is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
microtask_plan_draft.expected_behavior: MonacoEmbeddedCodeBlock leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoEmbeddedCodeBlock is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
microtask_plan_draft.expected_behavior: IntegrationValidatorEvidencePack leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: IntegrationValidatorEvidencePack is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Generated wiki page is treated as authority and drifts from source code/specs.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Wiki pages are projections only, carry source spans and generation metadata, and are marked stale when source hashes change.
failure_scenarios.verification: Tests mutate a cited source and prove the wiki page becomes stale until regenerated.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Context bundle omits the spec or WP that actually governs the code being edited.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Bundle compiler combines exact references, graph neighbors, keyword/vector retrieval, and dependency edges; debug view exposes omissions.
failure_scenarios.verification: Fixture task asks for code behavior and bundle must include linked spec topic and WP evidence.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Rich editor save format changes and old documents cannot load.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Version the document schema, write migrations, and keep round-trip fixtures for every block/embed/link type.
failure_scenarios.verification: Old fixture documents load after migrations and preserve content/links.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Auto-codeblock detection corrupts normal prose or mixed notes.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Use reversible conversion, confidence thresholds, user-visible correction, and no silent rewrite for low-confidence input.
failure_scenarios.verification: Paste fixtures cover prose, mixed prose/code, fenced code, JSON, and shell logs.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Embeds store machine-local absolute paths and break when the project moves.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Embeds reference artifact/media/source IDs or repo-relative/configured roots; portability checks reject hardcoded local paths.
failure_scenarios.verification: Move-root fixture proves documents and embeds still resolve through configured roots/artifact IDs.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Knowledge graph grows huge and becomes slower than raw file search.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Index incrementally, store hashes, add proper relational indexes, bound graph traversals, and expose slow-query diagnostics.
failure_scenarios.verification: Performance fixture with large file/edge counts checks index timings and query bounds.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Obsidian clone becomes a Markdown-only island disconnected from IDE/harness state.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Use the same PostgreSQL entities/edges/spans as code/spec/workflow indexing and treat Markdown as export only.
failure_scenarios.verification: A note link can resolve to a code symbol, file, folder, project, spec topic, and WP entity.

failure_scenarios.scenario: LLM relationship extraction invents links that look real.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Separate deterministic edges from inferred edges; inferred edges require evidence, confidence, generator metadata, and review/quarantine state.
failure_scenarios.verification: Unsupported generated relationship fixture is rejected or marked untrusted.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Implementation tries to introduce SQLite or a second durable database for the editor.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Activation contract repeats PostgreSQL-only constraint and tests should include the existing no-SQLite tripwire.
failure_scenarios.verification: No SQLite references or dependencies are added; no-SQLite tripwire remains passing.

failure_scenarios.scenario: One giant WP becomes too broad for reliable parallel implementation.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Refinement must lock a vertical slice and may split child WPs, but child WPs must preserve all three pillars and link back to this stub.
failure_scenarios.verification: Activation review checks MT boundaries and child WP preservation map if split.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Folded retrieval policy is implemented as generic hybrid_rag everywhere.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Make retrieval mode a first-class policy decision with exact/direct/graph outcomes and non_hybrid_reason telemetry.
failure_scenarios.verification: Fixtures for exact WP/spec/file/symbol loads prove direct_load or exact_lookup is selected and traced before hybrid_rag.

failure_scenarios.scenario: relationship_id values change across index runs and break graph trace reproducibility.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Derive relationship_id from stable edge identity inputs and persist it with source span/indexing run metadata.
failure_scenarios.verification: Repeated indexing of the same fixture produces identical relationship_id values; changed edge inputs produce controlled new IDs.

failure_scenarios.scenario: AI-ready evidence export cannot reconstruct rebuild/update history.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Export bounded lifecycle events, artifact manifests, provenance, retention metadata, and source hashes alongside retrieval evidence.
failure_scenarios.verification: Export/import fixture reconstructs artifact provenance and marks missing evidence explicitly.

failure_scenarios.scenario: ContextPack reuse depends on hidden in-memory cache decisions.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Record pack build/reuse/refresh/freshness events with pack hash, source hash, policy, and trace links.
failure_scenarios.verification: Replay/debug test explains why a pack was reused, refreshed, denied, or rebuilt without reading hidden runtime state.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Product code keeps legacy ModelManual naming and confuses future no-context models and users.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Migrate canonical product wording to UserManual and leave only explicit deprecated compatibility shims where needed.
failure_scenarios.verification: Search and tests fail on undeclared ModelManual, Model Manual, or model_manual references in product-code canonical surfaces.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Future model treats MemGraphRAG PDF or GitHub repositories as required implementation authority.
failure_scenarios.impact: No-context local models cannot continue after session compaction and Master Spec enrichment loses technical intent.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: WP-009 stores translated MemoryOntology/MemoryFact/MemoryPassage/ClaimLifecycle/ConflictResolution requirements and requires future enrichment to patch them into SPEC_CURRENT.

failure_scenarios.scenario: Tiptap or Monaco is integrated as an external editor/server dependency instead of bundled Handshake product code.
failure_scenarios.impact: Core editor behavior breaks offline/local operation and violates native dependency stance.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: MTs require package-lock governance, worker bundling, no-CDN proof, visual-debug evidence, and no-external-app validation.

failure_scenarios.scenario: CRDT scope is deferred too far and parallel model/editor writes lose data.
failure_scenarios.impact: Operator edits, model proposals, and graph claims can overwrite each other.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: MTs require actor/site IDs, CRDT draft state, EventLedger promotion, conflict UI, and negative parallel-write fixtures.

failure_scenarios.scenario: PDF or media source extraction partially fails but still enters memory as stable evidence.
failure_scenarios.impact: The memory system can form wrong facts from incomplete sources.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: MTs require extraction receipts, missing-text-layer states, transcript/OCR metadata, repair queues, and blocked stable promotion for partial evidence.

assumptions_to_attack: A PostgreSQL knowledge graph is the right durable substrate for Handshake project memory.
Generated wiki pages help models more than they confuse source-of-truth boundaries.
The rich editor can replace separate Monaco/text editor expectations without losing code-editing power.
Loom/Obsidian UI can be built on top of canonical typed entities rather than Markdown files.
A thin vertical slice can be built without blocking on full code intelligence for every language.
Model context rot is reduced by context bundle compilation plus evidence, not by dumping more raw files into prompts.
Folding older retrieval and evidence stubs into WP-KERNEL-009 does not hide broader cross-pillar requirements.
UserManual migration can be completed without breaking existing manual routes, IPC commands, tests, or model onboarding flows.
minimum_controls: Keep generated wiki pages and editor exports as projections, not source authority.
Require source spans/evidence on generated claims and context bundle items.
Require no-SQLite/PostgreSQL-only verification before implementation closeout.
Require visual frontend verification for editor and graph surfaces.
Require round-trip save/load tests before claiming rich editor implementation complete.
Require retrieval-mode, relationship_id, AI-ready evidence export, and ContextPack recorder tests before closeout.
Require product-code search proof for UserManual naming migration before closeout.

folded_legacy_stub_preservation.source_of_truth_rule: The folded stubs are deprecated/superseded and must not be activated directly. WP-KERNEL-009 is the active planning surface for their preserved scope.
folded_legacy_stub_preservation.conflict_policy: If a folded stub conflicts with WP-KERNEL-009, WP-KERNEL-009 wins unless the conflict would drop a major feature, hide a risk, weaken PostgreSQL-canonical storage, or remove deterministic retrieval/evidence behavior. In that case preserve the feature/risk as an explicit WP-KERNEL-009 requirement.
master_spec_basis: Project Brain: spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md Project Brain appendix and spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md Project Brain anchors define governed backend retrieval over AI-Ready Data with QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace and ContextPack reuse.
Semantic Catalog: spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md Semantic Catalog appendix and spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md catalog anchors define indexed backend routing contracts.
Retrieval mode policy: spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md requires none/direct_load/exact_lookup/graph_traversal/hybrid_rag and non-hybrid reasons in QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace.

AI-ready evidence/export and relationship IDs: spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md AI-Ready Data appendix and spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md relationship_id/graph_traversal anchors support bounded evidence and graph retrieval.
ContextPack recorder visibility: spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md and 12-end-of-file-appendices.md ContextPack anchors require visible build/select/refresh/freshness outcomes.
Loom/editor: spec-modules/07-user-experience-and-development.md rich editor inline token and Loom view anchors support the editor/navigation slice.

preserved_scope: Project Brain remains a governed runtime retrieval/notebook surface, not a vague RAG promise.
Project Brain query/runtime mapping must expose citations, context-compaction visibility, QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, and job/tool visibility.
Project Brain must distinguish discovery/synthesis from authoritative lookup and prefer fresh ContextPack reuse where appropriate.
preserved_acceptance: Project Brain runtime/job/tool visibility is explicit in the product implementation and validation evidence.
Project Brain links deterministically to ContextPacks, QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, SemanticCatalog, and the Project Knowledge Index.
preserved_risks: Project Brain becomes a generic semantic-search feature instead of a deterministic runtime surface.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: WP-KERNEL-009 owns the Project Knowledge Index slice; broader Project Brain behavior must be implemented only where needed to make that slice deterministic and citeable.

preserved_scope: Semantic Catalog remains a deterministic backend routing contract for tools, data surfaces, retrieval, and Spec Router planning.
Semantic Catalog entries may not remain hidden in prompts, helper labels, or UI-only descriptions.
The v2 stub was remediation for the earlier failed WP-1-Semantic-Catalog packet; this failure context remains preserved.
preserved_acceptance: Project Knowledge Index can index, query, and cite catalog entries as backend contracts.
Spec/router/retrieval planning can use catalog entries without prompt-only drift.
preserved_risks: Semantic Catalog stays a name-only dependency with no product-queryable backend representation.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: The empty v2 contract contributes remediation intent and name continuity; WP-KERNEL-009 supplies the implementation shape.

preserved_scope: Define bounded export anchors for AI-ready index artifacts and rebuild/update evidence.
Preserve manifest, retention, provenance, backend-portability, and reconstructability semantics for embedding, vector, keyword, and graph artifacts.
Context bundle and retrieval-debug evidence must reuse the canonical AI-ready export dialect instead of inventing a second export format.
preserved_acceptance: AI-ready index artifacts and lifecycle events are linked to bounded debug/export semantics.
Exported AI-ready evidence preserves stable provenance, retention, and PostgreSQL portability semantics.
Retrieval/debug/export packets can consume one canonical AI-ready evidence contract.
preserved_risks: Evidence exports expose inconsistent artifact subsets across backends.
Rebuild/update events are recorder-visible but not reconstructable from exported evidence bundles.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: WP-KERNEL-009 now owns the evidence semantics needed by project knowledge bundles; if later export work remains broader, it must reuse this preserved contract rather than restart the old stub.

preserved_scope: Knowledge graph edges require deterministic relationship_id values.
Extraction/indexing events must emit and persist relationship_id.
Hybrid retrieval and graph traversal must produce non-empty graph candidates for synthetic graph fixtures at minimum.
preserved_acceptance: Graph edges have stable relationship_id values.
Retrieval traces can cite relationship_id deterministically.
Synthetic graph tests prove non-empty graph candidates.
preserved_risks: Graph retrieval remains effectively stubbed because edges cannot be cited or reproduced.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: No conflict: relationship_id and graph candidates are core requirements for WP-KERNEL-009 typed edges and retrieval evidence.

preserved_scope: One governed retrieval-mode policy covers none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, and hybrid_rag.
QueryPlan and RetrievalTrace must record retrieval mode and non_hybrid_reason for exact-id, authority, freshness, and bounded-executor cases.

Prompt-to-Spec, Project Brain, Loom graph expansion, Work Packet loads, and Micro-Task context assembly must choose the cheapest authoritative retrieval path rather than defaulting to hybrid retrieval.
Dev Command Center, operator surfaces, replay/debug views, and context inspectors must expose retrieval posture and reasons.
preserved_acceptance: Exact identifiers and authoritative packet/spec state use direct_load or exact_lookup before hybrid_rag.
Graph traversal is preferred when bounded Loom/KG neighborhoods satisfy the task.
Retrieval evidence explains why hybrid retrieval was used or skipped.
preserved_risks: Everything becomes generic hybrid search, increasing cost and weakening authority boundaries.
Microtask and WP context assembly blur authoritative loads with advisory retrieval.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: WP-KERNEL-009 wins as the activation vehicle; broad retrieval policy remains preserved and must be implemented at least for all context-bundle, Loom/KG, Project Brain, Spec Router, WP, and MT paths touched by this packet.

preserved_scope: ContextPack build, reuse, refresh, freshness allow/deny, and require-rebuild decisions are first-class Flight Recorder evidence.
Pack hash, source hash, freshness policy, and job/spec-router/debug-bundle linkage must be stable and bounded.
Payloads must not dump full retrieval context by default.
preserved_acceptance: ContextPack lifecycle paths emit recorder-visible events with stable pack hashes and bounded policy metadata.
Replay/debug surfaces correlate pack decisions to jobs and spec-router flows without hidden runtime state.
Tests fail if pack-visibility events or hash linkage disappear.
preserved_risks: Context reuse depends on hidden cache decisions and cannot be replayed.
Full payload logging leaks too much retrieval context.
folded_stubs.conflict_resolution: WP-KERNEL-009 owns context-bundle observability for project knowledge; distillation-facing reuse must consume these recorder events rather than revive the old stub.

not_folded_here.reason: Referenced as a carried dependency by AI-ready evidence export, but not folded by this pass because the operator only approved the older knowledge/retrieval/editor-adjacent stubs and this scope should not silently absorb every transitive stub.

Referenced as a carried dependency by ContextPack recorder visibility; WP-KERNEL-009 must interoperate with it or implement the required trace persistence for its own paths, but the standalone ACE persistence stub is not deprecated by this fold.

handshake_native_dependency_contract.core_rule: Core WP-009 behavior must run as Handshake product code with Handshake-owned state, packaging, receipts, and validation.
allowed_external_runtime_inputs: explicitly gated CUI portable artifacts where an owning WP defines that boundary
bundled_product_libraries: Yjs or equivalent CRDT library for local human/AI draft-state semantics
Tree-sitter parser crates/grammars for code indexing
Rust and npm crates/packages locked and shipped through Handshake build manifests
forbidden_core_dependencies: external sync servers as core proof
SQLite fallback, cache authority, fixtures, or temp adapters for touched WP-009 paths
Redis/Valkey or other external cache authority
sidecar papers or chat memory as implementation authority
CDN-loaded editor workers or UI assets
handshake_native_dependency_contract.validation_rule: WP Validator and Integration Validator must reject PASS if core proof depends on a forbidden dependency or if a bundled library is not integrated through Handshake packaging, lockfiles, tests, and UserManual.

translated_memory_system_spec.source_of_truth_after_this_stub: WP-KERNEL-009 contract now carries the technical detail; future refinement/enrichment must patch this into SPEC_CURRENT and official MTs.

layers.name: MemoryOntology
layers.purpose: Stable schema memory for entity classes, relation classes, aliases, normalized terms, and promoted extraction patterns.
layers.promotion_rule: Schema terms start probationary and become stable only after evidence thresholds, conflict checks, or operator/spec approval.

layers.name: MemoryFact
layers.purpose: Evidence-backed factual claims and relationships with lifecycle state.

layers.name: MemoryPassage
layers.purpose: Source passages, OCR/transcript output, code/doc spans, and evidence snippets used to justify claims and retrieval.
layers.failure_rule: Missing text layers, partial extraction, and unsupported media produce receipts and repair queue items.

agent_jobs: ExtractionJob: parser/model extraction writes probationary claims and passages only.
ConflictDetectionJob: finds symbolic, temporal, alias, stale-source, and semantic conflicts.
ConflictResolutionJob: discards, refines, merges, temporal-qualifies, granularity-qualifies, or promotes claims with receipts.
BridgeEdgeJob: proposes graph bridge edges across fragmented subgraphs only when evidence and hub-suppression rules allow.
retrieval_rules: Exact IDs and authoritative paths use direct_load or exact_lookup before graph/hybrid retrieval.
Ontology/schema filters narrow graph candidates before ranking.
Graph traversal is bounded by depth, edge types, source authority, and hub suppression.
Passage fallback is explicit when structural graph candidates are missing, stale, contradicted, or low confidence.
Every answer/context bundle records QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, candidate evidence, rejected evidence, stale reasons, and non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
non_goals: Do not import MemGraphRAG as an external service.
Do not require a graph database outside PostgreSQL/EventLedger.
Do not treat LLM extracted triples as stable facts without Handshake evidence and conflict lifecycle.

enrichment_topics: Handshake-native memory system layers and claim lifecycle.
Tiptap/ProseMirror plus Monaco as fully integrated editor stack.
Loom/Obsidian projection over canonical knowledge records.
UserManual as model-facing and user-facing product documentation surface.
Backend navigation APIs for no-context local/cloud agents.
CRDT/EventLedger promotion bridge for rich docs, AI edit proposals, and graph mutation proposals.
Native dependency stance and bundled-library distinction.
Visual-debug requirements for editor, graph, retrieval, UserManual, and validation.
Negative fixtures and validator evidence matrix for WP-009.
stale_spec_targets_to_review: Any SQLite-as-index/cache/search authority language in current modules must be superseded for WP-009 by PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority.
Any ModelManual naming in current modules must migrate to UserManual or be declared as deprecated compatibility text.
Any external app/server/daemon default in current modules must be checked against v02.190/v02.191 Handshake-native dependency law.
Any paper/reference-only memory concept must be replaced by native product contracts before activation.

state_recovery_contract.reason: The operator frequently hits session/context limits; WP-009 must be recoverable by no-context local models.
each_mt_must_record: current MT ID, title, group, and dependencies
files changed and files intentionally not touched
PostgreSQL/EventLedger receipt IDs or expected receipt locations
HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable
remaining risks and next MTs unblocked
